definition: ``royal order or regulation'', by which certain persons or categories of persons (all called watĕk i jro?) are given the usufruct (see kilala) of people's taxes (contributions, services etc.) due to the king (see dṛwya haji). It seems that these persons themselves are also designated with the name rājawidhi (hence = maṅilala dṛwya haji). See also under rājawali, upaśubha.

citations.text: 2b.30: rājawidhi ṅaranya salwira niṅ wadwa haji, kaniṣṭhamadhyottama, ndan inubhaya sanmata de saṅ prabhu, inanugrahan taṅ wadwa wĕnaṅ amuktya ṅ dṛwya haji kilalanya ... definition: emphatic particle (cf kwa, kumwa). It often suggests the opinion of others (``so they say'', ``as you know'', ``naturally''), but by no means always. It is often added to another demonstrative word, emphatic particle or conjunction. For use in the parwas see Zoetmulder, De Taal van het Adiparwa and Bahasa Parwa. 1. (suggesting the thoughts of someone else) 2. (not suggesting another's opinion) 3. (some more examples of rakwa in combination with a demonstrative, etc)

3.133b: Srĕṅgara-Yudâparĕmpon baṅ wilis lagi anitihi kuda putih. The conjectural rendering of the problematic parĕmpu etc. is based on the assumption that the basic meaning is: to complete, to finish, to give the finishing touch. This would account for the frequent use with dress, ornaments, wood (``worked on'', as opposed to being in its unornamented or natural state), and make sense in the cases where it is used with sarwaphala (Td), pupuji Ww(A), awarah
and even in KK 57 (unless we have to assume there that marĕmpu = rĕmpu). A confirmation may be found in the use in bñw. (KBW) of aṅrĕmponi: ``to plough a second time''.
